summary: PR #2365 decompiled item/Pokemon and related Kirby code, replacing placeholders with C implementations across item files and updating headers/types to expose recovered signatures and item variable layouts. The PR body says 17 matched functions across 11 item/Pokemon files; the bot report confirms 12 new 100% matches plus 15 near-match/improvement entries, including several functions listed by the PR body.

key_files.role: Updated ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F21C0 prototype from void to float to match recovered return use.
Changed ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F21C0 to return ftPartGetRotX(GET_FIGHTER(gobj), 0).
Expanded item variable and attribute structs for Climbers Ice, Kirby Cutter Beam, and Sheik needle thrown fields.
Expanded item variable and attribute layouts for Lugia, Hitodeman/Staryu, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Patapata/Butterfree, and related item data.
Implemented Ice Shot functions it_802C16F8, itClimbersice_UnkMotion3_Phys, and it_2725_Logic90_HitShield.
Implemented it_80290938 Flipper spawning helper.
Implemented Staryu spawn logic and velocity/target adjustment helper it_802D48B0; refined local attributes.
evidence_refs: diff: ithitodeman.c placeholder replacements and attributes
bot_report: it_802D48B0 new match and it_2725_Logic24_Spawned improvement

key_files.role: Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS for it_802D48B0 with recovered void/f32 pointer and scalar parameter signature.
Implemented Blastoise spawned logic and used HSD_JObjSetScale for entry scale.
Implemented Kirby cutter beam setup using recovered Kirby angle helper, trig, velocity, direction, and initial position fields.
Implemented Lugia attack phase setup and replaced a raw/padded Vec3 access with typed lugia.x70 field use.
evidence_refs: diff: itlugia.c placeholder replacement and x70 field use

key_files.role: Added extern float declarations used by recovered Lugia logic.
Implemented Weezing floating-to-idle physics transition, including jobj rotation reset and hitlag/accessory setup.
Implemented Shadow Ball launch/update-related logic and split item var fields formerly represented as an array.
evidence_refs: bot_report: it_802C5B18 new match and itMewtwoshadowball_UnkMotion8_Anim improvement

key_files.role: Header changed for Shadow Ball work; exact content not visible in loaded diff excerpt.
Implemented/improved Octarok stage hazard physics according to PR body and bot report; exact diff not visible in loaded excerpt.
Implemented Butterfree/Patapata physics according to PR body and bot report; exact diff not visible in loaded excerpt.
Implemented Sheik thrown needle animation functions according to PR body and bot report; exact diff not visible in loaded excerpt.
evidence_refs: bot_report: itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion1_Anim and itSeakneedlethrown_UnkMotion4_Anim new matches

key_files.role: Implemented Tingle balloon/hover transition according to PR body and bot report; exact diff not visible in loaded excerpt.
Type header changed; exact line not visible in loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder markers with C implementations for multiple item/Pokemon functions, including Ice Shot, Staryu, Mewtwo Shadow Ball, Flipper, Blastoise, Kirby cutter beam, Lugia, and Weezing functions visible in the diff.
Updated item attribute and item-var structs in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h so recovered code can use typed fields rather than only pads or array indexing.
Recovered a fighter helper return value: ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F21C0 now returns the ftPartGetRotX result, and its header prototype was changed to float.
Added includes required by the newly recovered implementations, such as baselib/jobj.h, MSL/trigf.h, ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_Init.h, it/inlines.h, lb/lb_00B0.h, and math.h in touched files.
Bot report recorded matched-code improvement of +1852 bytes and matched-data improvement of +64 bytes, with 12 new 100% matches plus several near-100% function improvements.

smart_moves.text: Function signatures were updated where the recovered body proved a return value, shown by ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F21C0 changing from void to float and returning ftPartGetRotX.

Recovered item variable layouts were used to replace opaque padding or array-style access with named struct fields, for example lugia.x70 replacing a cast from x70_pad and mewtwoshadowball.x14/x18 replacing x10[1]-style indexing.
The Kirby cutter beam implementation reused the recovered Kirby attack angle helper rather than duplicating the fighter rotation access locally.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When an item function needs a value from a fighter helper, recovering the helper's true return type can unlock cleaner downstream item code and should be propagated to the owning header.

For item/Pokemon decompilation, partial recovery of ItemVars and dat attribute structs can be necessary for reviewable matches: fields used by recovered bodies were promoted from pads or arrays into typed f32, s32, Vec3, and pointer fields.
evidence_refs: diff: item code uses new fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: Use existing engine helpers for common object operations where visible: examples include HSD_JObjSetScale for Blastoise entry scale, lb_8000B1CC for joint/bone position extraction, and Item_80268E5C for item animation state changes.

naming_conventions.convention: Recovered item-var struct names follow existing item-specific naming, such as itKirbyCutterBeam_ItemVars and itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars, while uncertain fields remain offset-style names like x70, x8C, and xA4.
Where semantics are clear within a local struct, short descriptive fields were introduced for Kirby cutter beam state: angle, speed, dir, and init_pos.
Unproven function names remain address-style or motion/logic names, such as it_802D48B0, it_802C16F8, and it_2725_Logic90_HitShield.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow PAD_STACK use appeared in it_802C16F8 with PAD_STACK(24), implying stack-shape steering for that match or near-match; no explanatory verifier evidence beyond the bot improvement report is present.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Requires curator/reviewer caution under matching-tactics standards because the PR slice does not include objdiff rationale for PAD_STACK.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Some functions retained generated-looking local scoping and repeated GET_ITEM reloads, for example nested blocks in it_802C16F8 and separate item/item2 locals in itMatadogas_UnkMotion2_Phys, likely to preserve codegen shape.
evidence_refs: diff: itclimbersice.c nested blocks and GET_ITEM reload
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Evidence supports the source shape but not the author's exact intent; treat as matching tactic candidate, not a style standard.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Raw expanded jobj.h asserts were kept in itMatadogas_UnkMotion2_Phys before directly modifying jobj->rotate.y and conditionally calling ftCo_800C6AFC.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: This conflicts with the loaded header-inlines/assert macro cleanup preference unless local matching evidence required the expansion; PR slice only shows a 99.85% improvement, not full rationale.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The PR made small .sdata and .sdata2 improvements alongside function work; the bot report lists several data-section match improvements, but the slice does not show data ownership rationale.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.risk: Treat as report evidence only, not a reusable recommendation to chase data parity.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report showed matched code increased to 61.15% (+0.05%, +1852 bytes) and matched data to 37.78% (+0.01%, +64 bytes). It listed 12 new matches and 15 improvements in unmatched items.

follow_up_queries: Find later cleanup of PAD_STACK(24) in src/melee/it/items/itclimbersice.c it_802C16F8
Search for later replacement of expanded jobj.h __assert blocks in itMatadogas_UnkMotion2_Phys
Search PRs touching itKirbyCutterBeam_ItemVars angle speed dir init_pos
Search PRs after 2365 for final matches of it_8029BB90, itMatadogas_UnkMotion2_Phys, it_2725_Logic90_HitShield, it_80290938
Search for later semantic renames of itHitodeman_ItemVars x6C x70 x74 x78 x7C x80 x8C
Search for data-section ownership discussion for item .sdata/.sdata2 improvements in PR 2365

notes: PR metadata, changed-file list, PR body, and bot report are available.
Loaded raw_diff is truncated; exact changes for itmewtwoshadowball.h, itoldottosea.c, itpatapata.c, itseakneedlethrown.c, ittincle.c, and it/types.h are not fully visible in the excerpt.
No human review comments are present in the slice; review_feedback is limited to the automated decomp-dev bot report.

The PR body says 17 matched functions, while the bot report distinguishes 12 new 100% matches from 15 improvements/near-matches. This record preserves that distinction rather than treating every listed function as a confirmed full match.
No local build logs, objdiff artifacts, or author explanations for matching tactics such as PAD_STACK or expanded asserts are present.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Propagate recovered helper return types to headers and callers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2365 shows ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F21C0 changing from void to float, returning ftPartGetRotX, and then being used by item code to compute Kirby cutter beam velocity and direction. Candidate lesson: when a decompiled callee proves a return value, update the owning header and downstream call sites together.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover item-var fields to replace pads and array indexing used by matched bodies
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2365 expanded item variable and attribute structs in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h, then used those fields in item implementations. Candidate lesson: typed ItemVars and attribute fields can be a practical prerequisite for matching item/Pokemon behavior functions.

source_update_candidates.title: Expanded jobj.h asserts may indicate cleanup opportunities in item matches
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2365 kept raw __assert("jobj.h", 660/661, ...) expansions in itMatadogas_UnkMotion2_Phys while achieving only a near-match improvement in the bot report. This may be useful as an example for the existing header-inlines/assert cleanup standards: expanded JObj assertions should be reviewed against HSD_JObj helpers or inline forms when evidence supports them.
source_update_candidates.reason: Routed as a source update candidate because the slice suggests a standards example, but lacks enough evidence to promote a new standard or claim the original source form.

source_update_candidates.title: PAD_STACK in near-matched item functions needs explicit rationale
source_update_candidates.text: it_802C16F8 includes PAD_STACK(24), and the bot report lists it as 99.88% rather than a full new match. This can be cited as a cautionary example under matching-tactics-need-evidence: stack padding should remain narrow and should carry verifier evidence if retained.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible standards example only; PR evidence does not include the author's rationale or objdiff artifact for the PAD_STACK.

source_update_candidates.title: Item/Pokemon behavior work spans src/melee/it/items plus shared item headers
source_update_candidates.text: This PR's item/Pokemon decompilation touched many src/melee/it/items/*.c files and required shared layout updates in src/melee/it/itCharItems.h and src/melee/it/itCommonItems.h. Curator may record this as a path-scope fact if consistent with existing corpus.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR supports this path relationship, but path facts are source-owned and should be curated separately.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR body's functional descriptions as verified game semantics beyond this PR slice; they are author-provided summaries and some diff sections are truncated.
Do not infer that all 17 listed functions fully matched from the bot report; the report separates 12 new matches from near-match improvements.
Do not treat PAD_STACK, expanded __assert blocks, generated-looking local scopes, or repeated GET_ITEM reloads as accepted style based on this PR alone.
Do not infer reviewer intent or human approval rationale; no human review comments were included.
